phases.name: Initial Prototype & Core Components
phases.description: This collection of repositories represents the initial stages of the tinymachines project. It encompasses the foundational elements of the project – a small knowledge net (`knowysynet`), a vector comparison tool (`vectl`), an OCR LLM processor (`fkj`), and a system for managing screen sessions (`scream`).  The developers were focused on establishing the core functionality and testing the basic architecture before moving on to more complex features.

phases.name: Core Infrastructure & Tooling
phases.description: This repository represents the foundational elements of the tinymachines ecosystem – the core development tools and infrastructure necessary for building and maintaining the project. It encompasses the initial creation of key components like Python scripts, generators, and basic utilities, laying the groundwork for the subsequent development of more complex features and systems.

phases.name: Development of Core Components & Experimental Projects
phases.description: This repository collection represents the initial stages of the tinymachines project ecosystem, focusing on foundational components and experimental projects. It encompasses the development of EEG monitoring, a bootstrap guide for ARM assembly, a Python agentic language called Spydr, and various web scraping tools like Sovereign and SPIDr.  The project is characterized by iterative development and experimentation across different programming languages and architectures.

phases.name: Tinymachines Core Development
phases.description: This repository represents the foundational development of the tinymachines project, focusing on establishing the core infrastructure and building the initial structure for the system. It encompasses initial setup, service management, and a critical groundwork for the subsequent development of the project’s key features, including the docker-less installation and service management scripts.

milestones.title: Initial Project Setup with Structure and Dependencies
Add Success_Rate to All Return Paths in Test Harness

phases.name: Hotbits - Initial Development
phases.description: This repository represents the initial development of the Hotbits project, focusing on building a TypeScript-based SMS gateway wrapper for the tinymachines ecosystem. It includes core functionality and a foundational framework for future expansion.

milestones.title: Implement complete SMS gateway wrapper with CLI and MCP server
Add CLAUDE.md for SMS gateway wrapper project
Add HTML output default for stdout and compilation support
Add user agent, cookie jar, and stream output support

phases.name: Development Pipeline
phases.description: This repository represents the core development pipeline for the tinymachines project, encompassing key features used in the initial stages of development. It’s a collection of modules focused on foundational functionality, including a database system, a user interface, and essential tooling for the project’s core components.

phases.name: Core Infrastructure & Initial Development
phases.description: This phase represents the foundational development of the tinymachines ecosystem, focusing on establishing the core components and initial infrastructure. It includes creating the initial repositories for key tools like resume, 6502, cascade, tgmi, scout, rustforhaters, and ripgrep – all crucial for the project's functionality. This period establishes the basic structure and functionality of the project.

phases.name: AI-Powered Educational Content Platform Development
phases.description: This project represents the evolution of the tinymachines ecosystem, focusing on building a robust AI-driven platform for educational content generation. It begins with the initial development of deox, a Python script for automating PDF generation, and progresses through the creation of gnome, collidr, spicy, orator, esp32, and gene-pool, all centered around AI coding agent systems and educational content creation.  The project encompasses a range of functionalities, including AI-assisted lecture generation, curated archives, and a focus on creating a dynamic and evolving learning environment.

milestones.title: gene-pool - AI Coding Agent System Prompts

repos.name: scout
repos.description: The Scout network scanner is a shell-based tool that performs various tasks such as automated key installation, drive-time guide, run verification report, and more. The repository has undergone significant updates since its initial release on November 22, 2025, with recent commits adding features like SSH access enumeration, distributed deployment system, and service reconnaissance system. The current version is v1.0.0.
